response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
When it comes to handling conversations about healthcare, specifically COVID vaccines, I think it's important to approach them with a clear head and an open mind. Having two PhD parents definitely made me realize that education doesn't have to be formal; curiosity can lead you down some interesting paths.

As someone who works as a waiter/waitress in New York City, I've had plenty of chances to engage people from all walks of life about various topics—including the pandemic and vaccines. It's not always easy because emotions run high sometimes, but my agreeableness score is pretty high, meaning I try to be empathetic without being pushy.

I spend an hour a day on social media these days, which keeps me somewhat updated with what people are saying online about COVID issues. But honestly, I prefer face-to-face conversations because they're more personal and you can really gauge the other person's reaction that way. That said, my low extraversion score means I don't enjoy being the center of attention too much; I'd rather listen than lead discussions.

When someone brings up COVID vaccines or any healthcare topic near me while working, like if a customer asks about side effects during dinner reservation confirmations—wait no, that's not right—but when they do ask questions naturally during service interactions. I always try to answer honestly based on what little formal education I have but rely more on my own research.

For instance, one night someone was curious about getting vaccinated themselves and asked me if there were any downsides beyond common side effects like sore arm or tiredness the next day. Since they mentioned being curious—like mine—I shared some of what I'd learned from reliable sources online but also admitted when I didn't know everything.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Step-by-step derivation: Starting with personal background (curious agnostic, two PhD parents), then considering how work environment influences discussion management skills due to frequent exposure in urban setting. Addressed emotional aspects through agreeableness trait and balanced information intake via daily social media use but preferring real-life interactions.
citations: Personal research on reliable health websites
